title: Update Secret
content:
In this example we will update a secret with a new phrase "This is a new big secret!" as the name `demosecret`.
=== "Kelvin UI" Click on the Secrets menu under Orchestration. Find the secret you want to update and then click on the Edit icon { width=20 height=20 } in that row. You can now add your new value to the secret name. You will not see the secret again, so make sure you type it correctly. You can update the value with a new one again, but you will not be able to see the original value. When updated you can click the **Save** button to save it. Congratulations, you now have updated a value in an existing secret variable. === "Kelvin API" ```bash title="API cURL Example" linenums="1" curl -X "POST" \ "https:///api/v4/secrets/demosecret/update" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer " \ -H "Accept: application/json" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "value": "This is a new big secret!" }' ``` The response will look like this; ```json title="API cURL Example Response" linenums="1" { "name": "demosecret", "created_by": "krn:user:example@kelvin.ai", "created": "2025-11-24T07:05:46.789693Z", "updated": "2025-11-24T07:06:03.694621Z", "updated_by": "krn:user:example@kelvin.ai" } ``` === "Kelvin API Client (Python)" ```python title="API Client (Python) Example" linenums="1" from kelvin.api.client import Client # Login client = Client(url="https://", username=""}) client.login(password="") # Update Secret response = client.secret.update_secret(secret_name="demosecret", data={"value": "Nh9Noq%QWNaJim%uAe9r"}) print(response) ``` You will see the output like this; ```bash title="API Client (Python) Example Response" linenums="1" name='demosecret' created=datetime.datetime(2025, 11, 24, 7, 5, 46, 789693, tzinfo=TzInfo(UTC)) created_by='krn:user:example@kelvin.ai' updated=datetime.datetime(2025, 11, 24, 7, 17, 56, 245169, tzinfo=TzInfo(UTC)) updated_by='krn:user:example@kelvin.ai' ``` === "Kelvin SDK CLI" ```bash title="Kelvin SDK Command Line Example" linenums="1" kelvin secret update --value="This is a new big secret!" demosecret ``` If everything went well you will get the confirmation response like this; ```bash title="Kelvin SDK Command Line Output" linenums="1" [kelvin.sdk][2025-11-24 07:21:26][I] Updating secret "demosecret" on the platform [kelvin.sdk][2025-11-24 07:21:26][I] Fetching credentials from keyring [kelvin.sdk][2025-11-24 07:21:28][R] Secret "demosecret" successfully updated on the platform ```

code_examples.code:
curl -X "POST" \
    "https://<url.kelvin.ai>/api/v4/secrets/demosecret/update" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer <Your Current Token>" \
    -H "Accept: application/json" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
            "value": "This is a new big secret!"
        }'
code_examples.title: API cURL Example

code_examples.code:
from kelvin.api.client import Client

    # Login
    client = Client(url="https://<url.kelvin.ai>", username="<your_username>"})
    client.login(password="<your_password>")

    # Update Secret
    response = client.secret.update_secret(secret_name="demosecret", data={"value": "Nh9Noq%QWNaJim%uAe9r"})

    print(response)
code_examples.title: API Client (Python) Example

code_examples.code: name='demosecret' created=datetime.datetime(2025, 11, 24, 7, 5, 46, 789693, tzinfo=TzInfo(UTC)) created_by='krn:user:example@kelvin.ai' updated=datetime.datetime(2025, 11, 24, 7, 17, 56, 245169, tzinfo=TzInfo(UTC)) updated_by='krn:user:example@kelvin.ai'
code_examples.title: API Client (Python) Example Response

code_examples.code: kelvin secret update --value="This is a new big secret!" demosecret
code_examples.title: Kelvin SDK Command Line Example

code_examples.code:
[kelvin.sdk][2025-11-24 07:21:26][I] Updating secret "demosecret" on the platform
    [kelvin.sdk][2025-11-24 07:21:26][I] Fetching credentials from keyring
    [kelvin.sdk][2025-11-24 07:21:28][R] Secret "demosecret" successfully updated on the platform
code_examples.title: Kelvin SDK Command Line Output
